summary.summary: Refactors Trio's alert/notification pipeline to route all LoopKit device and glucose alerts through a single TrioAlertManager; introduces a user-configurable glucose-alarm list (urgent-low / low / high / forecasted-low / carbs-required), tiered device-alarm configs (Critical / Time‑Sensitive / Normal) with per-tier snooze, a shared Day/Night window, catalog-based pump alert classification, and unified in-app banner + UserNotification scheduling and snooze handling. Adds a bundled sound library and a critical-audio fallback player, a time‑sensitive UN entitlement, and replaces the legacy Not-Looping monitor with a re-armed delayed alert. Also surfaces CGM-owned glucose alerts in a dedicated “Handled by CGM App” section and adds a Carbs Required glucose alarm type. The change includes accompanying unit tests and UI changes to the Settings modules.
summary.userFacingImpact: Users will see alerts routed through a unified alert manager and new Settings sections (Glucose Alarms, Device/Pump & CGM Alarms, Day & Night Window). Glucose alarms are now a configurable list (including a new "Carbs Required" type) with per-alarm thresholds, schedule, sound and snooze; device/pump alerts are configured by severity tier with per-tier sound / Override‑Silence behavior. If a CGM app owns glucose alerts, Trio suppresses those and shows them in a "Handled by CGM App" section with the companion app named and deep-linked when available. In-app banner behavior and background UserNotifications are unified (foreground → banner, background → UN), and snooze actions are centralized so quick-snooze actions (phone/watch/banner/lock-screen) behave consistently. A bundled set of CAF tones and a CriticalAlertAudioPlayer fallback are added; the app also includes the com.apple.developer.usernotifications.time-sensitive entitlement so time‑sensitive alerts can pierce Focus. The Not‑Looping alert is now a delayed(20m) re-armed LoopKit alert that skips when open-loop/manual basal/suspended. Existing stored settings for older per-category notification flags are removed and glucose alarm entries are backfilled on first launch. (See sources for details. Human review advised before release to confirm localization, entitlement handling, and any store-migration behavior in specific builds.)
